It provides unified management and coordination support to provincial and capital city statistical offices within the National Statistical Office’s Chancellery.

Objective.title: Ensure compliance with laws and regulations on public service and implement a sectoral level human resources policy.
Enhance the skills and professional qualifications of civil servants in the statistical system through training and capacity building.
Support the implementation of relevant policy documents and programmes; and provide information services to leadership.
Provide centralized information and methodological support to provincial and capital city statistical offices.
Fulfil other responsibilities assigned by the relevant laws and regulations.

duties.title: Ensure implementation of the Law on Public Service and other related laws, regulations, decrees, and decisions at the sectoral level; and provide unified management of human resources activities.
Develop, implement, and evaluate the sectoral human resources policy document.
Develop and implement, in a phased manner, programmes and plans aimed at improving working conditions, health, and social security arrangements for civil servants.
Promote knowledge exchange and job rotation among sector level civil servants to improve their skills and adaptability.

Receive, review, and submit proposals and materials for state orders, medals, sectoral awards, and other recognition for civil servants and citizens; handle the nomination and decision making process; and maintain a centralized record.
Establish and maintain a human resources information system; and carry out digitalization, updating, and tracking of personnel data.
Organize the approval and performance evaluation of the Government Civil Service workplans for NSO staff, and exercise oversight over their implementation.
Organize the activities of the State Civil Service Sector Council for the statistical sector.
Monitor the utilization of working hours and introduce corrective measures where necessary.
Identify training needs; develop and implement training programmes and curricula.
Organize the participation of civil servants in training and capacity building activities.
Coordinate nominations of civil servants for participation in external programmes and initiatives; submit proposals to management for decision making.
Collaborate with higher education institutions that train specialists in statistics and economics; and support internships and practical training for students in relevant fields.
Organize leadership advisory meetings; develop and implement plans for the follow up and implementation of the decisions taken.

Prepare and finalize the agenda for expanded executive meetings; organize the preparatory work, minutes, and record keeping; ensure implementation of decisions; and report on the execution of the workplan.
Coordinate and approve the performance plans of provincial and capital city statistical offices; and consolidate their performance evaluation reports.
Provide centralized information and methodological support to provincial and capital city statistical offices.
Prepare proposals and draft amendments to improve, update, and replace internal regulations and instructions governing the section’s mandate.
Implement and comply with the information security management system’s policies, rules, and procedures in all operational activities and ensure their effective application.

In collaboration with the Office’s Risk and Internal Audit Unit, identify, assess, and manage risks associated with the section’s mandate; implement preventive measures; and organize training and methodological support.
Ensure compliance with the Law on Public Service, the Law on Labour, the Law on Archives, and other relevant legislation.
Carry out the department’s and section’s internal operations; implement tasks assigned by the Office’s leadership; and participate in other activities organized by the Office.

gazar.title: Finance and Investment Division
gazar.goals: To plan and report the budget within the framework of the General Budget Administrator in accordance with relevant laws, regulations, and procedures; to manage financial and accounting activities; and to ensure the conditions necessary for the normal operation of the sector.

Objective.title: To prepare necessary budgetary and investment calculations and organize the process of budget approval;
To conduct financial and accounting activities in compliance with laws and regulations;
To ensure conditions for the normal operation of the sector;
To implement relevant policy documents and programs, provide management with information, and participate in internal operations.

duties.title: To implement proper financial and budgetary management of the sector, including budget planning, expenditure, reporting, and reconciliation; to conduct analysis; and to organize financial control and regulation effectively;
To adhere to accounting policy documents and ensure their implementation;

To manage financing of projects and programs implemented at the sector level within approved budgets, authorize expenditures appropriately, prepare financial reports, coordinate project reporting to international organizations, and conduct audits;
To plan, organize, and report procurement activities at the sector level in accordance with relevant legislation;
To identify the sector’s internal requirements for materials, equipment, and spare parts, develop a consolidated supply plan, obtain approval, and ensure implementation;

To manage the storage and protection of fixed and current assets, materials, and spare parts according to established procedures; maintain regular accounting records, keep fixed asset registers, perform inventories and asset transfers in a timely manner, utilize resources efficiently, and prepare timely reports;
To determine the sector’s financial needs in accordance with the Law on Budget, and to develop and implement the required budget proposal;

To maintain accounting records in accordance with relevant procedures, prepare sector-level and Office-level financial statements on an accrual basis within the established timeframe, submit them to relevant organizations, and arrange for audit evaluations;
To generate and report sector and Committee budget execution data and make adjustments to the approved budget as required;

To calculate and distribute employee salaries, leave, and other benefits in accordance with regulations, and submit social insurance and tax reports to relevant authorities within the prescribed timeframe;

To supervise financial activities of local statistical offices, work closely with accountants responsible for finance, and provide necessary advice, information, and guidance to ensure unified management;
To ensure transparency of budget, financial, and procurement activities in accordance with the Law on Transparent Accounts and other relevant legislation;

To consolidate financial proposals related to the statistical sector in line with national socio-economic development plans and submit them to the central government agency responsible for financial matters;
To create conditions for the normal daily operations of the organization;
To develop proposals for improving or updating rules and procedures within the scope of responsibilities;
To implement information security management policies and procedures in operations and ensure compliance;
To manage operational risks within responsibilities and collaborate with the Internal Audit and Risk Management Division (IARMD);
To identify, assess, and manage potential risks related to unit functions, and collaborate with the IARMD in organizing training and methodological support;
To ensure compliance with the Law on Civil Service, the Labor Law, the Law on Archives, and other relevant legislation;
To implement internal operations of the department and division, execute tasks and directives from Committee management, and participate in other activities organized by the Office.

gazar.title: Internal Audit, Monitoring and Evaluation Department
gazar.goals: This department is responsible for conducting internal audits and monitoring evaluation activities on the operations of the National Statistical Office’s central departments, sections, and regional units; identifying and assessing operational risks; and reporting findings to the Office’s Director. It aims to improve the efficiency and effectiveness of all activities, ensure continuity of operations, and provide risk management guidance and recommendations to prevent and mitigate potential risks.

Objective.title: Implement risk management arrangements at the statistical sector level.
Conduct performance, compliance, and financial audits in accordance with international internal audit standards and the “State sector internal audit methodology.”
Carry out internal control and review activities across all stages of the production of official statistics.
Assess the implementation of policy documents, laws and regulations, decrees, and decisions; and conduct monitoring evaluation and performance assessment of the organization’s activities.

duties.title: In collaboration with relevant departments and units, identify and assess potential operational risks to the organization’s activities, outputs, services, and operating environment; exercise oversight; report findings; and provide risk management guidance and recommendations.

Prepare and finalize medium term and annual internal audit and monitoring evaluation workplans; implement internal audits and reviews across the Office’s departments, sections, central level units, and regional offices; and present recommendations and conclusions to the Office’s leadership for decision making and assignment of corrective actions.

Monitor the implementation of recommendations arising from internal audits and reviews, as well as from the State Auditor’s reports and official instructions, and check the resolution of identified irregularities.

Cooperate with external audit bodies and financial control agencies on the audit of the organization’s financial statements and other operations; support the correction of audit identified errors and violations; and monitor the implementation of audit recommendations.

Carry out monitoring evaluation and performance assessment of the implementation of policy documents, laws and regulations, decrees, and decisions; and of the annual performance reports of the Office and of provincial and capital city statistical units; provide analytical findings and recommendations.

Provide technical and methodological support to national auditors; prepare proposals for their appointment and dismissal for consideration by the Office’s Director; and organize the issuance and withdrawal of the auditors’ credentials in accordance with the approved procedures.

Develop, update, and oversee the implementation of internal regulations, instructions, guidelines, and manuals relevant to the department’s mandate; and prepare proposals to improve, update, and replace these documents.
Implement and comply with the information security management system’s policies, rules, and procedures in all operational activities and ensure their effective application.
Ensure compliance with the Law on Public Service, the Law on Labour, the Law on Archives, and other relevant legislation.

In collaboration with the Office’s Risk and Internal Audit Unit, identify, assess, and record risks associated with the department’s mandate; implement measures to reduce risk levels and to prevent future incidents; and organize reporting, training, and methodological support for senior management and governing bodies.
Carry out the department’s internal operations; implement tasks assigned by the Office’s leadership; and participate in other activities organized by the Office.

gazar.title: Information Technology and Data Engineering Department
gazar.goals: This department is responsible for defining, at the sectoral level, a unified information technology (IT) policy; for building the government’s big data infrastructure; for developing and maintaining the centralized national statistical data repository; and for ensuring data integrity, coherence, and interoperability. It designs and implements statistical data processing software systems and is charged with delivering and managing the technological solutions that support the entire statistical production process.

Objective.title: Develop and modernize the statistical sector’s IT and data infrastructure; and progressively introduce and embed advanced and emerging technologies.

Link the government agency data repositories with the national statistical data system; design the technical and architectural solutions required to establish the government level big data repository; and ensure data security, storage integrity, and protection.

Provide technical and technological support to ensure seamless integration across all stages and sub stages of the General Statistical Business Process Model (GSBPM); manage data security, storage, and protection; and lead the adoption and support of sector level innovations based on machine learning and artificial intelligence.

duties.title: Define and implement a unified IT policy for the statistical sector and continuously improve the underlying technological infrastructure.

Provide technology enabled support for the inter stage and inter substage linkages within the General Statistical Business Process Model and ensure that new advanced technologies are embedded into all stages of the statistical production process.

Within the framework of building the national big data repository, connect and integrate the data repositories of relevant government agencies with the national statistical data system; establish the statistical sector data infrastructure; create a unified metadata repository; implement an administrative data integration system; and develop a central data catalog.
Design, pilot, and deploy software systems and tools for statistical data processing and survey/census operations.
Support related units in the integrated processing of statistical indicators, census data, and survey data within the unified statistical data processing platform.
Ensure the stable and reliable operation of the organization’s external and internal networks and of its hardware and equipment; and improve system availability and performance.
Be responsible for the use, service, security, storage, and protection of the statistical data repository.
Enhance the skills and capacities of civil servants in handling big data and advanced data processing applications; and provide methodological and technical support.
Conduct regular self assessments within the framework of quality management and implement measures to improve both operational performance and data quality.

Develop and implement organizational and managerial measures to ensure compliance with the sector level information security standards; and manage the day to day operations of the information security management system.
Prepare proposals and draft amendments to improve and update the internal regulations and instructions governing the department’s mandate.
Implement and comply with the policies, rules, and procedures of the information security management system in all operational activities and ensure their effective application.

In collaboration with the agency’s Risk and Internal Audit Unit, identify, assess, record, and manage risks related to the department’s mandate; implement risk mitigation and preventive measures; and organize reporting, training, and methodological support to strengthen risk awareness and institutional resilience.
Ensure compliance with the Law on Public Service, the Law on Labour, the Law on Archives, and other relevant legislation.
Carry out the department’s internal operations; implement tasks assigned by the agency’s leadership; and participate in other activities organized by the agency.

gazar.title: Information Technology Division
gazar.goals: Within the framework of the department’s mandate, this section is responsible for implementing a unified, sector level policy for software and hardware systems; for developing and maintaining the national statistical information technology infrastructure; for managing the government level big data environment; and for delivering and supporting the technological solutions required for statistical data processing. It also designs and deploys the software systems used to collect, process, and disseminate statistical information, and ensures the secure, reliable, and uninterrupted operation of the organization’s internal and external networks.

Objective.title: Develop and modernize the national statistical information technology infrastructure; improve the use of internal and external networks and hardware and equipment; and ensure secure, reliable, and stable data processing operations.

Within the framework of building the national big data environment, develop and implement the technical and technological solutions required to link the data repositories of government agencies with the national statistical system; and be responsible for the use, service, and availability of the centralized data repository.
Ensure the integrated processing of statistical data, census data, and survey data within the unified statistical processing environment.
Enhance the computer literacy and technical skills of civil servants; and provide methodological guidance and support.
Support the implementation of relevant policy documents and programmes; provide information services to management; and participate in the department’s internal operations.

duties.title: Implement the unified information technology policy for the statistical sector and continuously improve the technological infrastructure.

Provide technology enabled support for all stages of the General Statistical Business Process Model; ensure seamless integration; and introduce new technologies across the statistical production chain.
Ensure the continuous, reliable, and secure operation of the national statistical information system.

Maintain and strengthen secure cross institutional networks (e.g., VPN) and online systems; enhance the security and reliability of communications and data exchange environments; and periodically renew hardware and equipment.
Study and introduce advanced technologies for receiving, processing, and disseminating statistical data and official reports.
Introduce cost effective and advanced technologies for data submission and transmission from regional offices.
Design, pilot, and deploy software systems for processing monthly, quarterly, and annual statistics, census data, and survey data.
Establish and maintain the centralized statistical database; enrich it regularly; and ensure high level confidentiality, security, and backup.
Use only licensed, official software systems to protect data confidentiality and security.

Develop and implement management and organizational measures to comply with sector level information security standards; and manage the day to day operations of the information security management system.
Create technical conditions for delivering statistical information to domestic and international users in a timely, accessible, and low cost manner.
Establish the technical capacity for remote working in emergency or exceptional situations.
Improve the design, usability, and reliability of the institution’s website and maintain it as a regularly updated information portal.
Study internationally recognized software products and statistical tools; introduce them at the sector level; and organize training for staff.
Ensure the stable operation of technical and audio visual equipment in meeting and training rooms; and organize virtual meetings and training sessions.
Provide civil servants with timely technical, software, and maintenance support.
Conduct regular internal quality assessments and implement measures to improve operational performance and data quality outcomes.
Prepare proposals and draft amendments to improve and update the internal regulations and instructions governing the section’s mandate.
Implement and comply with the information security management system’s policies, rules, and procedures in all operational activities and ensure their effective application.

In collaboration with the agency’s Risk and Internal Audit Unit, identify, assess, and manage information technology related risks; implement risk mitigation and preventive measures; and organize training and methodological support.
Ensure compliance with the Law on Public Service, the Law on Labour, the Law on Archives, and other relevant legislation.
Carry out the department’s and section’s internal operations; implement tasks assigned by the agency’s leadership; and participate in other activities organized by the agency.

gazar.title: Data Engineering Division
gazar.goals: Within the framework of the department’s mandate, this section is responsible for linking and integrating government agency data repositories with the national statistical data systems, and for building a unified, quality assured data infrastructure that can be used for analysis and research. It develops and maintains a centralized data repository and a metadata repository that support evidence based decision making and data driven analytics.

Objective.title: Within the framework of building the national big data environment, integrate and connect the data repositories of government agencies with the national statistical data systems; carry out data cleaning and curation; and build a unified, fit for use analytical data repository and associated metadata repository.
Continuously enrich the metadata of the centralized data repository and develop a comprehensive data catalog.
Prepare, via a statistical data laboratory, datasets for users; ensure that data are anonymized or otherwise de identify individuals; and maintain strict oversight of data access and data usage.
Support the implementation of relevant policy documents and programmes; provide information services to management; and participate in the department’s internal operations.

duties.title: Define and regularly update the technical and quality requirements for data and data repositories in line with current and anticipated user needs.
Monitor and oversee the implementation of data sharing and confidentiality arrangements across the system.
Develop and maintain standardized coding guidelines and best practice instructions for data repositories, and promote their consistent application across statistical and government agencies.
Establish the statistical sector data infrastructure required to link and integrate other government agency data repositories with the national statistical data systems.
Prepare analytical datasets for users through the statistical data laboratory; ensure that personal identifiers are removed or anonymized; and monitor and control data access and data usage.

In collaboration with other departments and units, transform integrated multi source data into big data format structures and, where appropriate, convert big data outputs into standardized statistical information formats.
Evaluate other government and statistical agencies’ data repositories and provide methodological guidance and recommendations for improvement.
Refine and maintain metadata standards; oversee data entry and curation processes; and provide guidance for the continuous enrichment and quality control of the centralized data repository.
Assess and analyse metadata from statistical and government agencies; and provide methodological guidance and recommendations.
Develop documentation and governance frameworks for the organization’s spatial (geographic) data infrastructure and ensure its coordinated management.

Lead, in collaboration with domestic and international partners, projects and programmes to modernize the data infrastructure, technology, metadata, and spatial data systems, and ensure broad involvement of other units.
Prepare proposals and draft amendments to improve and update the internal regulations and instructions governing the section’s mandate.
Implement and comply with the information security management system’s policies, rules, and procedures in all operational activities and ensure their effective application.

In collaboration with the agency’s Risk and Internal Audit Unit, identify, assess, and manage information technology and data related risks; implement risk mitigation measures; and organize training and methodological support.
Ensure compliance with the Law on Public Service, the Law on Labour, the Law on Archives, and other relevant legislation.
Carry out the department’s and section’s internal operations; implement tasks assigned by the agency’s leadership; and participate in other activities organized by the agency.

gazar.title: Economic Statistics Department
gazar.goals: To organize censuses and surveys in economic, price, environmental, and disaster statistics; develop methodologies aligned with international standards; compile and analyze data; and disseminate results.

Objective.title: To compile key economic and price indicators;
To produce and analyze sectoral statistics;
To improve methodologies across sectors.

duties.title: To improve, develop, and implement reports, forms, guidelines, classifications, methodologies, and methods for economic, price, environmental, and disaster statistics in accordance with international standards, and to calculate and analyze the relevant indicators.
To collect annual data from economic sectors, calculate value added, and submit the results for integration into the national accounts.
To calculate the price indices required for the estimation of Gross Domestic Product (GDP).
To lead and organize economic and price surveys and censuses, consolidate the results, and perform the relevant calculations.
To regularly maintain, update, and enhance the integrated database and metadata database of economic, environmental, and price statistical indicators.
To introduce and implement statistical quality management.

To incorporate directions for the development of economic, price, environmental, and disaster statistics into the medium-term statistical development program and strategic plans, and to implement the activities specified therein.
To cooperate with the unit responsible for the business register and censuses in preparing the coverage of censuses and surveys of economic sectors.
To ensure the reliability and accuracy of economic, price, environmental, and disaster statistical data and to conduct monitoring.
To use additional data sources in producing economic, price, environmental, and disaster statistical information.
To automate the processing of reports, data, censuses, and surveys, and take measures to simplify data processing procedures.
To perform analytical calculations and studies upon request from the State Great Khural, the Government, other government institutions, and users.
To ensure compliance with the Civil Service Law, the Labor Law, the Archives Law, and other relevant legislation and regulations.
To implement and ensure compliance with the policies of the Information Security Management System in daily operations.

To identify and assess potential risks related to the functions of the department, register them in the risk database, implement preventive and risk mitigation measures, and organize reporting, training, and methodological support in cooperation with the unit responsible for operations and risk management, as well as submit proposals for decision-making to the relevant council and management.

To implement the internal activities of the department, carry out tasks assigned by the leadership of the National Statistics Office, and participate in other activities organized by the National Statistics Office
